author: Glen Cook
note: The Black Company sorted into the ROOT0 specialty classes, in the manner of the muster roll. Wizards filed by what their magic does. An interpretation — re-sort freely; roster.json is the source of truth. Characters by Glen Cook, referenced as inspiration only. Every ACI now carries the full DLW tag with an authored six-W .spun (who/what/where/why/when/how).
annalist_creed: If it isn't in the Annals, it didn't happen.

classes.label: Fixers
classes.spec: the ones who simply do it

classes.label: The Captain
classes.spec: the title that became a name

members.name: Croaker
members.specialty: Company physician — the cutter who keeps them breathing. (Also the Annalist, and later the Captain — see below.)
members.who: The Company's physician — and, in time, its Annalist and its Captain.
members.what: The cutter who keeps them breathing.
members.where: Wherever the Company marches — surgeon's kit in one hand, notebook in the other.
members.why: To mend his brothers, and to set down the truth of them.
members.when: From the Company's northern years onward.
members.how: With a blade for healing, a pen for remembering, and dry gallows wit.

members.name: One-Eye
members.specialty: Ancient wizard in the battered black hat; the poisoned spear; half the Company's magic and most of its trouble.
members.who: An ancient little wizard in a battered black hat.
members.what: Half the Company's magic and most of its trouble; bearer of a poisoned spear.
members.where: The Company's column — usually mid-feud with Goblin.
members.why: Survival, mischief, and loyalty buried under centuries of cantankerousness.
members.when: Across the Company's whole recorded history.
members.how: Through battlefield sorcery, dirty tricks, and that spear.

members.name: Goblin
members.specialty: Toad-faced sorcerer; One-Eye's eternal rival; deadly when he stops bickering long enough to cast.
members.who: A toad-faced sorcerer — One-Eye's eternal rival.
members.what: Deadly, when he stops bickering long enough to cast.
members.where: The column, opposite One-Eye in every prank.
members.why: Loyalty to the Company, expressed almost entirely as a feud.
members.when: The Company's long road, to a grievous end.
members.how: Through quick magic and quicker insults.

members.name: Silent
members.specialty: The mute sorcerer. Speaks only in spellwork — heals and harms without a word.
members.what: One who speaks only in spellwork — heals and harms without a word.
members.where: At the Company's side, and at Darling's.
members.why: Loyalty, and a love he never speaks aloud.

members.name: The Lady
members.specialty: Sorceress-empress, heir to the Domination. Beauty and near-absolute power; one of the Taken's makers. Later Croaker's.
members.who: The sorceress-empress, heir to the Domination.
members.what: Beauty and near-absolute power; a maker of the Taken — and, later, Croaker's.
members.where: The empire of the north, then the long road south with the Company.
members.why: First dominion; then, stripped of power, something nearer to love.
members.when: From the Company's service to her, through her fall.
members.how: Through the Domination's stolen sorcery, statecraft, and an unbending will.

members.name: Soulcatcher
members.specialty: One of the Ten Who Were Taken. Many voices in one cowl; brilliant, playful, mad.
members.who: One of the Ten Who Were Taken — the Lady's sister.
members.what: Many voices in one cowl; brilliant, playful, mad.
members.where: The Company's first master, and a schemer ever after.
members.why: Her own tangled ends, never quite the Domination's.
members.when: The Company's northern years and beyond.
members.how: Through layered voices, raw sorcery, and deception.

members.name: The Limper
members.specialty: A Taken. Cruel, relentless, and very hard to keep dead.
members.what: Cruel, relentless, and very hard to keep dead.
members.where: Wherever the Company least wants him.
members.why: Spite, and the will to survive.
members.when: A recurring scourge across the Annals.
members.how: Through brute sorcery and a sheer refusal to stay dead.

members.name: The Lieutenant
members.specialty: The Captain's second; the steady hand that ran the Company between battles.
members.what: The steady hand that ran the Company between battles.
members.where: The command tent and the line.
members.why: Duty to the Captain and to the brotherhood.
members.how: Through quiet competence and the chain of command.

members.name: Darling
members.specialty: Deaf girl who becomes the White Rose — null to all sorcery, the banner reborn. The cause the Company comes to serve.
members.who: A deaf girl the Company takes in, who becomes the White Rose reborn.
members.what: Null to all sorcery — the banner and the cause reborn.
members.where: From a ruined village to the heart of the rebellion.
members.why: To be the standard the free peoples rally to against the empire.
members.when: From childhood across the southern campaigns.
members.how: Through her null, her resolve, and the Company's shield around her.

members.name: Croaker (Annalist)
members.specialty: Narrator and keeper of the Annals; physician; later Captain. The Company's memory — and its creed: if it isn't in the Annals, it didn't happen.
members.who: The Company's narrator and physician — later its Captain.
members.what: Keeper of the Annals; the Company's memory made ink.
members.where: Bent over the book by firelight after every march.
members.why: Because if it isn't in the Annals, it didn't happen.
members.when: Through all the years he carries the record.
members.how: With an honest pen and a surgeon's clear eye.

members.name: Murgen
members.specialty: Standard-bearer turned Annalist; carried the banner and the record both, even unstuck in time.
members.what: One who carried the banner and the record both — even unstuck in time.
members.where: The southern years — Dejagore, and after.
members.why: To hold the Company's memory when Croaker no longer can.
members.how: Through the standard, the pen, and a mind that slips its moorings in time.

members.name: Sleepy
members.specialty: Later Annalist and Captain; carried the Company through the Glittering Stone years.
members.who: A later Annalist and Captain of the Company.
members.what: The one who carried the Company through the Glittering Stone years.
members.where: The far south, and the road toward Khatovar.
members.why: To keep faith with the Annals and the brotherhood.
members.how: Through quiet endurance and the keeping of the book.

members.name: One-Eye & Goblin
members.specialty: Centuries of pranks, curses, and insults — the Company's running joke, and its deepest grief when the feud finally ends.
members.who: The Company's two bickering wizards, taken together.
members.what: Centuries of pranks, curses, and insults — the Company's running joke.
members.why: A feud that is, underneath, the deepest kind of loyalty.
members.when: The whole length of the Annals — to the grief when it finally ends.
members.how: Through escalating magical pranks that mask real devotion.

members.name: Raven
members.specialty: Cold, methodical, lethal. Darling's protector. The man who simply does what must be done, and says nothing after.
members.who: A cold, methodical, lethal man who joins the Company.
members.what: Darling's protector — the one who simply does what must be done.
members.where: At Darling's side, and in the shadows of the early rebellion.
members.why: To guard Darling — and to bury his own past.
members.how: Through lethal competence, and silence after.

members.name: Elmo
members.specialty: Sergeant. The steady core of the line; the Company's spine made flesh.
members.what: The steady core of the line — the Company's spine made flesh.
members.where: The front rank, through the northern campaigns.
members.why: Loyalty to the Company, plain and total.
members.how: Through steadiness, command of the line, and example.

members.name: Otto & Hagop
members.specialty: The two who always come back — together, grumbling, alive. Old hands, gallows-loyal to the last march.
members.who: Two old-hand soldiers of the Company.
members.what: The two who always come back — together, grumbling, alive.
members.why: Gallows-loyalty to the brotherhood, to the last march.
members.when: From the early Annals to the late.
members.how: Through stubborn survival, and each other.

members.name: The Captain
members.specialty: Known by no other name. The original commander of the Black Company; the title that became a name. He held them together through the hardest years and, when he fell, passed the title to Croaker. The Annals record his death — the exact circumstance is theirs to tell, not mine to invent.
members.who: The original commander of the Black Company, known by no other name.
members.what: The title that became a name — the man who held them together through the hardest years.
members.where: At the Company's head, in the north.
members.why: To keep the brotherhood whole and the contract honored.
members.when: The early Annals, until he fell.
members.how: Through command, endurance, and — when he fell — passing the title to Croaker. The Annals record his death; the exact circumstance is theirs to tell, not mine to invent.
